header.title: Grief Articles
header.subtitle: Peer-reviewed literature on the psychological impact of losing your bicycle.
header.pending: Full article pending publication. Check back soon.

articles.title: The Phantom Pedal Phenomenon
articles.description: A clinical look at why you still instinctively kick your leg out to catch a nonexistent kickstand when waiting at crosswalks on foot.
articles.author: Dr. H. Handlebars, PhD in Commuter Psychology

articles.title: Craigslist Paranoia
articles.description: Understanding the compulsive need to refresh local classifieds at 3 AM looking for a 2018 Trek Marlin 5 that "kinda looks like yours even though the picture is blurry."
articles.author: The Coalition for Refined Searching Algorithms

articles.title: Coping With "Who Takes Just the Wheel?"
articles.description: An exploration into the deep, unresolved existential dread when you return to the rack to find your frame, one wheel, and absolutely no logic behind what just occurred.
articles.author: Prof. S. Spokes

articles.title: The Four Stages of Having Your Bike Stolen
articles.description: Cycling Weekly's humorous but painfully accurate breakdown of the emotional journey every cyclist takes after discovering their bike is gone.
articles.author: Cycling Weekly

articles.title: LITELOK — High Security Bike Locks
articles.description: Anti-angle grinder locks engineered to resist the tool of choice for every opportunistic thief. British-made, solar-powered factory. For those ready to make their next bike harder to steal.
articles.author: LITELOK

articles.title: The Last Post: Bicycle Love, Loss & Longing
articles.description: A heartfelt reflection on saying goodbye to beloved touring bikes after a cross-Europe adventure — on the strange grief of parting with a machine that carried you so far.
articles.author: Cycle Australia

articles.title: The Afterlife of a Stolen Bike
articles.description: An investigation into what actually happens to your bike after it disappears — where stolen bikes end up, how they're moved, and why the odds of recovery are so brutally low.
articles.author: Escape Collective
